𝗩𝗢𝗜𝗖𝗘𝗧𝗬𝗣𝗥 | Offline voice-to-text dictation for enhanced productivity on macOS and Windows.  📈 Growing!

┉┉┉┉┉┉┉┉┉┉┉┉┉┉┉┉┉┉┉┉┉┉┉┉

𝗧𝗘𝗖𝗛 𝗦𝗧𝗔𝗖𝗞: 🦀 Rust 
🎯 𝗨𝗦𝗘 𝗖𝗔𝗦𝗘: Offline voice-to-text dictation for enhanced productivity on macOS and Windows.
📅 Created: Jul 2025 • Updated: Jul 2026

┉┉┉┉┉┉┉┉┉┉┉┉┉┉┉┉┉┉┉┉┉┉┉┉

✨ 𝗞𝗘𝗬 𝗙𝗘𝗔𝗧𝗨𝗥𝗘𝗦 ✨

✅ System-wide hotkey for quick recording
✅ Automatic text insertion at cursor position
✅ Works in any app
✅ Local transcription by default
✅ Multiple AI model sizes for accuracy/speed tradeoffs
✅ Support for 99+ languages
✅ Hardware acceleration (Metal on macOS, GPU on Windows)
✅ Privacy-focused with local processing
✅ Optional AI enhancement via Groq/Gemini
✅ Clean UI with menubar integration
✅ Auto-updates


┉┉┉┉┉┉┉┉┉┉┉┉┉┉┉┉┉┉┉┉┉┉┉┉

📊 𝗣𝗥𝗢𝗝𝗘𝗖𝗧 𝗦𝗧𝗔𝗧𝗦

⭐⭐⭐ 425 𝘀𝘁𝗮𝗿𝘀
🍴 73 𝗳𝗼𝗿𝗸𝘀
🔍 2 𝗼𝗽𝗲𝗻 𝗶𝘀𝘀𝘂𝗲𝘀

┉┉┉┉┉┉┉┉┉┉┉┉┉┉┉┉┉┉┉┉┉┉┉┉

📝 𝗔𝗕𝗢𝗨𝗧 𝗧𝗛𝗜𝗦 𝗣𝗥𝗢𝗝𝗘𝗖𝗧

Voicetypr is an open-source, offline AI-powered voice-to-text dictation tool for macOS and Windows. It offers local transcription for privacy, supports numerous languages, and can optionally enhance transcriptions using cloud AI services.

┉┉┉┉┉┉┉┉┉┉┉┉┉┉┉┉┉┉┉┉┉┉┉┉

🔗 𝗟𝗜𝗡𝗞𝗦
• 𝗚𝗶𝘁𝗛𝘂𝗯: https://github.com/moinulmoin/voicetypr

┉┉┉┉┉┉┉┉┉┉┉┉┉┉┉┉┉┉┉┉┉┉┉┉

👉 GitHub Open Source: https://t.me/GitHub_Open_Source

┉┉┉┉┉┉┉┉┉┉┉┉┉┉┉┉┉┉┉┉┉┉┉┉


🏷️ 𝗧𝗔𝗚𝗦
#reactjs #shadcn_ui #tailwindcss #tauri #voice_assistant

𝗖𝗢𝗠𝗠𝗢𝗡𝗙𝗢𝗥𝗠𝗦 | To automate the process of transforming static PDF documents into interactive, fillable forms, making data entry and document processing more efficient.  🌟 Rising Fast!

◈◈◈◈◈◈◈◈◈◈◈◈◈◈◈◈◈◈◈◈◈◈◈◈

𝗧𝗘𝗖𝗛 𝗦𝗧𝗔𝗖𝗞: 🐍 Python 
🎯 𝗨𝗦𝗘 𝗖𝗔𝗦𝗘: To automate the process of transforming static PDF documents into interactive, fillable forms, making data entry and document processing more efficient.
📅 Created: Sep 2025 • Updated: Jul 2026

◈◈◈◈◈◈◈◈◈◈◈◈◈◈◈◈◈◈◈◈◈◈◈◈

✨ 𝗞𝗘𝗬 𝗙𝗘𝗔𝗧𝗨𝗥𝗘𝗦 ✨

✅ Automatic PDF to fillable form conversion
✅ CLI and API for form preparation
✅ Supports FFDNet-S and FFDNet-L models
✅ Option to keep existing fields
✅ Option to use signature fields
✅ Configurable inference parameters (device, image size, confidence)


◈◈◈◈◈◈◈◈◈◈◈◈◈◈◈◈◈◈◈◈◈◈◈◈

📊 𝗣𝗥𝗢𝗝𝗘𝗖𝗧 𝗦𝗧𝗔𝗧𝗦

⭐⭐⭐⭐ 1.1K 𝘀𝘁𝗮𝗿𝘀
🍴 146 𝗳𝗼𝗿𝗸𝘀
🔍 8 𝗼𝗽𝗲𝗻 𝗶𝘀𝘀𝘂𝗲𝘀

◈◈◈◈◈◈◈◈◈◈◈◈◈◈◈◈◈◈◈◈◈◈◈◈

📝 𝗔𝗕𝗢𝗨𝗧 𝗧𝗛𝗜𝗦 𝗣𝗥𝗢𝗝𝗘𝗖𝗧

CommonForms is a Python package that automatically converts standard PDF documents into fillable forms by detecting and marking form fields. It provides both a command-line interface (CLI) and an API for this conversion process, leveraging pre-trained models like FFDNet-S and FFDNet-L.

◈◈◈◈◈◈◈◈◈◈◈◈◈◈◈◈◈◈◈◈◈◈◈◈

🔗 𝗟𝗜𝗡𝗞𝗦
• 𝗚𝗶𝘁𝗛𝘂𝗯: https://github.com/jbarrow/commonforms

◈◈◈◈◈◈◈◈◈◈◈◈◈◈◈◈◈◈◈◈◈◈◈◈

𝗙𝗢𝗥𝗧 | To provide users with advanced control over network connections on their Windows machines, enhancing security and managing bandwidth usage.  🔥 Super Hot!

✱✱✱✱✱✱✱✱✱✱✱✱✱✱✱✱✱✱✱✱✱✱✱✱

𝗧𝗘𝗖𝗛 𝗦𝗧𝗔𝗖𝗞: 🔨 C++ 
🎯 𝗨𝗦𝗘 𝗖𝗔𝗦𝗘: To provide users with advanced control over network connections on their Windows machines, enhancing security and managing bandwidth usage.
📅 Created: Dec 2014 • Updated: Jul 2026

✱✱✱✱✱✱✱✱✱✱✱✱✱✱✱✱✱✱✱✱✱✱✱✱

✨ 𝗞𝗘𝗬 𝗙𝗘𝗔𝗧𝗨𝗥𝗘𝗦 ✨

✅ Flexible network rules per application or global
✅ Wildcards in application path names
✅ Parent process based rules
✅ Filter by SvcHost.exe service names
✅ Speed limit application groups
✅ Blocklists via "Zones"
✅ Saves traffic statistics
✅ Graphical display of the bandwidth
✅ Standalone firewall with own driver


✱✱✱✱✱✱✱✱✱✱✱✱✱✱✱✱✱✱✱✱✱✱✱✱

⭐⭐⭐⭐ 3.3K 𝘀𝘁𝗮𝗿𝘀
🍴 227 𝗳𝗼𝗿𝗸𝘀
🔍 68 𝗼𝗽𝗲𝗻 𝗶𝘀𝘀𝘂𝗲𝘀

✱✱✱✱✱✱✱✱✱✱✱✱✱✱✱✱✱✱✱✱✱✱✱✱

📝 𝗔𝗕𝗢𝗨𝗧 𝗧𝗛𝗜𝗦 𝗣𝗥𝗢𝗝𝗘𝗖𝗧

Fort Firewall is a robust and user-friendly firewall application for Windows, designed to provide granular control over network traffic. It allows users to create flexible rules based on applications, addresses, ports, and protocols, with features like speed limiting and blocklists.

✱✱✱✱✱✱✱✱✱✱✱✱✱✱✱✱✱✱✱✱✱✱✱✱

• 𝗚𝗶𝘁𝗛𝘂𝗯: https://github.com/tnodir/fort

𝗖𝗢𝗡𝗧𝗔𝗜𝗡𝗘𝗥-𝗗𝗜𝗘𝗧 | Optimizing Docker images for size, security, and best practices using AI-driven analysis and automated fixes.  🔥 Super Hot!

♦♦♦♦♦♦♦♦♦♦♦♦♦♦♦♦♦♦♦♦♦♦♦♦

𝗧𝗘𝗖𝗛 𝗦𝗧𝗔𝗖𝗞: 🐹 Go 
🎯 𝗨𝗦𝗘 𝗖𝗔𝗦𝗘: Optimizing Docker images for size, security, and best practices using AI-driven analysis and automated fixes.
📅 Created: Nov 2025 • Updated: Jul 2026

♦♦♦♦♦♦♦♦♦♦♦♦♦♦♦♦♦♦♦♦♦♦♦♦

✨ 𝗞𝗘𝗬 𝗙𝗘𝗔𝗧𝗨𝗥𝗘𝗦 ✨

✅ AI-powered Docker image and Dockerfile analysis
✅ Actionable optimization advice
✅ Automatic Dockerfile fixing (`--auto-fix`)
✅ Support for 12+ AI providers (OpenAI, Anthropic, Ollama, etc.)
✅ CLI tool for direct usage
✅ MCP server for AI agent integration (e.g., in editors like Cursor)
✅ Tools for AI agents: analyze_dockerfile, analyze_image, get_optimization_advice, get_image_summary


♦♦♦♦♦♦♦♦♦♦♦♦♦♦♦♦♦♦♦♦♦♦♦♦

📊 𝗣𝗥𝗢𝗝𝗘𝗖𝗧 𝗦𝗧𝗔𝗧𝗦

⭐⭐ 68 𝘀𝘁𝗮𝗿𝘀
🍴 3 𝗳𝗼𝗿𝗸𝘀
🔍 0 𝗼𝗽𝗲𝗻 𝗶𝘀𝘀𝘂𝗲𝘀

♦♦♦♦♦♦♦♦♦♦♦♦♦♦♦♦♦♦♦♦♦♦♦♦

📝 𝗔𝗕𝗢𝗨𝗧 𝗧𝗛𝗜𝗦 𝗣𝗥𝗢𝗝𝗘𝗖𝗧

Container Diet is a Go-based CLI tool that optimizes Docker images using AI. It analyzes Dockerfiles and images to provide actionable advice and automatically generate optimized versions, aiming to reduce image size and improve security.

♦♦♦♦♦♦♦♦♦♦♦♦♦♦♦♦♦♦♦♦♦♦♦♦
